name: App Store Connect MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Apple App Store Connect API enabling AI agents to manage iOS/macOS app distribution, review app metadata, manage TestFlight beta testing, access sales reports, and interact with App Store Connect programmatically — automating routine app publishing and management tasks.
use_cases: Managing app metadata and screenshots from app publishing automation agents
Monitoring App Store review status and responding to rejections from DevOps agents
Managing TestFlight beta builds and tester groups from QA automation agents
Pulling sales and subscription analytics from reporting agents
not_for: Teams without Apple Developer Program membership
App submission automation without review of content (App Store policies apply)
best_when: An iOS/macOS developer or team wants AI agents to automate App Store Connect tasks — metadata management, TestFlight, review monitoring, and analytics — reducing manual portal work.
avoid_when: You develop for Android only. App Store Connect requires Apple Developer Program ($99/year). Some App Store actions still require human review and judgment.

auth.notes: App Store Connect API key (Issuer ID + Key ID + private key). JWT-based authentication. Keys scoped to specific capabilities (Sales, Developer, etc.).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — App Store Connect API is free for Apple Developer Program members
pricing.paid_tiers: None for API; Apple Developer Program $99/year required
pricing.notes: App Store Connect API is free but requires $99/year Apple Developer Program. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: App Store Connect API rate limits apply; typically adequate for management tasks

known_agent_gotchas: App Store Connect API private key is sensitive — store securely and never expose to agent outputs
Some App Store actions (submissions, rejections) have compliance and legal implications
API capabilities are scoped — ensure API key has required permissions for intended operations
App Store Connect API can change with new Apple developer releases — check compatibility
Community implementation — less tested than official Apple tooling
agent_readiness.security_notes: API private key must be secured (never committed, never logged). App Store Connect operations can affect published app distribution. Review agent actions before enabling autonomous submissions.

security_score_components.security_notes: Private key auth. App Store operations affect real app distribution. Secure key management essential.
